submissions.title: Gamified Labs for Network Security Education: A Controlled Study
submissions.abstract: Cybersecurity education increasingly relies on hands-on practice and gamified learning to improve retention and engagement. This study examines the effectiveness of a capture-the-flag style lab environment in teaching network security concepts to undergraduate students at a mid-sized university. We deployed a controlled environment where students completed a series of challenges covering reconnaissance, vulnerability scanning, and secure configuration over a six-week period. Pre- and post-assessments measured conceptual understanding and self-reported confidence. We also collected telemetry on time spent and attempt patterns. Results indicate significant gains in both assessment scores and confidence, with the largest improvements among students who had no prior security coursework. Student feedback highlighted the value of immediate feedback and the ability to retry without penalty. We discuss implications for curriculum design and the trade-offs between guided versus open-ended lab structures. Limitations include a single-institution sample and the use of a custom platform that may not generalize to other tools. Future work will compare this approach to traditional lecture-and-homework formats and examine long-term retention. Our findings support the use of gamified, lab-based modules as a core component of introductory cybersecurity education. In addition, we surveyed instructors who adopted similar labs and found that integration with existing courses required minimal support once initial setup was complete. We provide recommendations for institutions considering hands-on security labs and suggest extensions for advanced topics such as forensics and secure coding. The study was approved by the institutional review board and all participants provided consent. Data and materials are available upon request. We conclude that well-designed gamified labs can effectively supplement traditional cybersecurity instruction and recommend their inclusion in undergraduate programs. A replication package including lab designs and assessment instruments will be made available to support adoption at other institutions.
submissions.keywords: cybersecurity education, gamified learning, capture-the-flag, network security, undergraduate

submissions.title: Privacy-Preserving Analytics for Healthcare Security Monitoring
submissions.abstract: This proposal outlines a research project in the area of applied cybersecurity, with emphasis on methodology, expected outcomes, and relevance to the field. We describe the problem context, related work, and our approach. The project aligns with institutional priorities and is designed to be completed within the proposed timeline with the requested resources.
submissions.keywords: privacy, healthcare security, analytics, monitoring, data protection
